response.justification: Candidate has notable geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map work, which directly aligns with the routing/navigation domain. However, the core required skill—modern C++ or Rust—is largely absent. The only C/C++ exposure is a brief six-month stint at National Instruments roughly a decade ago, and there is no Rust experience listed. Recent work has been predominantly TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Could be worth a screening call given the geospatial domain fit and willingness to work across stacks, but the language-stack gap is significant for a senior IC role.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map)
Polyglot background with low-latency systems work in DLang at Dunnhumby (high-throughput micro-services)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing focus
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience (only ~6 months in 2014–2015) and no Rust
Recent roles are full-stack web (TypeScript/Node), not systems-level pipeline engineering
No demonstrated experience with large-scale distributed data pipelines or routing tile generation
